meaning: n.<英><贬>禄虫(指为个人私利而任命的官吏)
examples: The word "placeman" is often used contemptuously.
Thee word "placeman" is often used contemptuously.
With parliamentary elections due in September, Ahmed Wali remains a crucial placeman.
But appointing a placeman from among their ranks is not the answer to their complaints.
President Viktor Yanukovych - elected in April 2010 - has said EU membership is a "strategic aim" but has been accused of being a placeman for pro-Russian tendencies in the country.
